question: How do I enable 2FA / two-factor authentication?
answer: Go to Settings → Security → 2FA → Enable. Scan the QR code with Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator, Authy, or 1Password. Enter the 6-digit code to confirm pairing. From then on, every login and every withdrawal asks for the 6-digit code. Strongly recommended — it's the single best defence against account takeover. If you lose your 2FA device later, our support team can help you recover via identity verification.

question: What if I lose my 2FA device?
answer: Contact support immediately: support@alfardanq9.com or the support ticket in Settings. We'll verify your identity via a live video KYC re-check (typically 1-2 UAE business hours during business days) and reset 2FA on your account. Once reset, you'll be prompted to pair a new authenticator on next login. During the reset window, withdrawals are temporarily blocked for safety.

question: I got a suspicious email — is it from Al-Fardan?
answer: We ONLY send from @alfardanq9.com domains. We NEVER: (1) ask for your password, (2) ask for your 2FA code by email/phone/chat, (3) ask you to 'verify' by clicking a link to a login page, (4) ask for a wallet private key or seed phrase. If you got an email asking for any of those, it's phishing — forward the full email (with headers) to security@alfardanq9.com and we'll investigate. When in doubt, go to the portal directly via your bookmark, not by clicking an email link.

question: How are withdrawal addresses secured?
answer: Three layers: (1) 2FA required on every withdrawal. (2) Optional whitelisting — pre-approve destination addresses with a 24-hour cooldown so same-day address changes can't be used to exfiltrate funds. (3) Manual admin review on every non-whitelisted withdrawal before Fireblocks broadcasts the transaction — a human double-checks the address and amount. For institutional accounts we also support multi-sig withdrawal approval requiring a director's co-sign.

question: What if my phone or laptop is stolen?
answer: Immediately: (1) use another device to sign in to the portal, go to Settings → Security → Sign Out All Sessions. That kills every active session across every device. (2) Change your password. (3) If 2FA was on the stolen device, follow the lost-2FA recovery flow. (4) Consider whitelisting your known-good addresses so any attempt to withdraw to a new address triggers the 24-hour cooldown. Funds in custody are not accessible without both password + 2FA, so even a stolen logged-in session can't drain you without 2FA.

question: How do I set a strong password?
answer: Our minimum: 8 characters, 1 number, 1 symbol. Our recommendation: 16+ characters, generated by a password manager (1Password, Bitwarden), never reused across sites. Do NOT reuse your password from any other site — credential-stuffing attacks use breach databases from other services. Use a password manager so you don't have to remember. Enable 2FA on top of the password as an additional layer.

question: How do you prevent hacks and account takeovers?
answer: Multiple layers: (1) Passwords hashed with bcrypt (cost 12). (2) 2FA on login + withdrawals. (3) Rate-limited login attempts (5 failures = 15-minute lockout). (4) Session tokens expire after 7 days. (5) Every withdrawal reviewed by a human admin before Fireblocks broadcasts. (6) IP-based anomaly detection — login from a new country triggers an email alert. (7) Whitelisted withdrawal addresses prevent address-swap attacks. (8) Fireblocks MPC custody means even a full CRM compromise can't move your crypto unilaterally.

question: Common crypto scams to watch out for
answer: (1) 'Support impersonators' — anyone contacting you claiming to be Al-Fardan support on Telegram, Discord, or WhatsApp is a scammer. We only support via our portal + email. (2) 'Airdrop' scams promising free tokens — legitimate airdrops never ask for your seed phrase. (3) 'Romance' scams pivoting to 'let me help you invest' — always a scam. (4) Fake app-store clones of reputable wallets — only install from the official source linked on the project's own website. (5) Seed-phrase theft via fake Ledger/Trezor firmware updates. When in doubt, ask us via the portal chat or email.

question: How do I verify an email is really from Al-Fardan?
answer: (1) Sender domain: we only send from @alfardanq9.com (not @al-fardan.com, al-fardanq9.co, etc — those are impersonators). (2) Real emails never ask for your password, 2FA code, or seed phrase. (3) Don't click links — open the portal directly from your bookmark and check your in-app notifications, which always mirror the email. (4) Check the email signature matches our official layout. When in doubt, forward the email to security@alfardanq9.com for verification.

question: How do I report a phishing attempt?
answer: Forward the full email (with original headers intact — use 'Forward as Attachment' in most email clients) to security@alfardanq9.com. For phishing text messages, forward the SMS to support with the sender's number. For phishing phone calls, note the caller's number and report. We track every reported attempt and share the indicators with our security partners. Reporting helps protect the whole client base.

question: Do I need a seed phrase for my Al-Fardan account?
answer: No. You don't have a seed phrase with us — we use MPC (multi-party computation) custody, not single-key cold storage. Your crypto is controlled by key shards distributed across Fireblocks infrastructure; no single party (including us) can unilaterally move funds. You recover your ACCOUNT via email + 2FA, not via a seed phrase. If anyone claiming to be from Al-Fardan asks for a seed phrase, they're a scammer — report immediately.

question: Can I recover a lost account?
answer: Yes. Use 'Forgot Password' on the login page — we'll email a 6-digit OTP to reset. If you've lost access to your email too, contact support@alfardanq9.com from any address and provide: (1) your full name, (2) account email, (3) approximate account creation date, (4) a recent transaction ID if you have one, (5) government ID. We verify identity via video KYC (1-2 UAE business hours) and restore access. We never recover funds — we recover ACCESS to your existing account.

question: What's SIM-swap fraud and am I at risk?
answer: SIM-swap is when an attacker convinces your phone carrier to transfer your number to their SIM, letting them intercept SMS 2FA codes. Al-Fardan uses TOTP 2FA (authenticator app), NOT SMS 2FA, specifically because SMS is vulnerable to SIM-swap. Your 2FA codes are generated offline on your device — no attacker can intercept them without physically having your phone. If you've seen other services with SMS 2FA, switch to TOTP.

question: How do you protect against account takeover?
answer: Login layer: bcrypt password + TOTP 2FA + rate-limited attempts + IP-anomaly email alerts. Action layer: withdrawals require 2FA again + manual admin review + optional 24h-cooldown whitelisting. Custody layer: Fireblocks MPC means even a full CRM compromise can't unilaterally move funds — the policy engine requires a co-sign from a hardware-secured Fireblocks policy signer we hold offline. Three independent breaches would be needed to drain funds.

question: Is my connection to the portal encrypted?
answer: Yes — TLS 1.3 on all portal and API traffic, with HSTS enforcement. We score A+ on SSL Labs. The chatbot you're talking to right now is also TLS-encrypted end-to-end. Mixed content is blocked at the browser level, and we don't load any third-party scripts that could intercept traffic. You can verify by clicking the padlock in your browser's address bar.
